info.name: User Order Service API Tests
info.description:
Full API test suite for user-order-service (Postgres/Supabase clone of server/user-order-creation). Run with the matching environment file.

IMPORTANT — this hits the REAL PRODUCTION SUPABASE DATABASE (same DB as admin-service/tracking-service/payment-verification-service). Requests in this collection are deliberately scoped to validation-error paths, 404s on obviously-fake IDs (e.g. 'THIS-ORDER-DOES-NOT-EXIST-XYZ'), and read-only lookups — nothing here creates a real user, order, or affiliate by default.

A few endpoints unavoidably write a low-value log row even on 'empty' requests (fingerprint/collect). That's noted on the request itself.

EXCEPTION — 'Auth — Requires Real Fixtures' contains a Register + Login happy-path pair that runs against ONE fixed fixture account (collection variables 'testEmail' / 'testPassword', defaulted to a dummy @user-order-service.test address). POST /api/auth/register upserts by email (see index.js), so re-running this is idempotent — it never creates a new row after the first run, it just updates the same one. The Login request stores the returned JWT into the 'authToken' collection variable, which is what lets the rest of the 'Requires Real Fixtures' folders (wallet/affiliate) exercise their real 200 happy paths instead of just asserting 401.

The 'Requires Real Fixtures' folder is for authenticated routes (wallet/affiliate) that need a real JWT. Set the collection variable 'authToken' (a real token from POST /api/auth/login) to exercise the happy path; left blank, those requests still run and correctly assert 401.

KNOWN BUG (not a test mistake): PUT and DELETE /api/user-orders/:orderNumber currently return 500 'syntax error at or near "LIMIT"' instead of 404, because the original MySQL code used `UPDATE ... LIMIT 1` / `DELETE ... LIMIT 1`, which Postgres does not support. The db-adapter.js shim does not currently strip LIMIT from UPDATE/DELETE statements. The tests for these two routes assert the CORRECT expected behavior (404) and will fail (red) until that's fixed — this is intentional, so the suite catches the regression rather than enshrining the bug.

exec: // Does NOT create/update a user — fails validation before any DB write.
pm.expect(pm.response.json()).to.have.property('error', 'Date of birth, nationality and country of residence are required');

item.name: Forgot Password — Nonexistent email → 200 (no email sent)

exec: // Always returns success even for unknown emails (anti-enumeration) — no reset token
// row is written and no email is sent unless the user actually exists.
pm.expect(pm.response.json()).to.have.property('error', 'Password must be at least 6 characters long');

item.name: Register — Fixed fixture account → 201 (creates/updates real user)

exec: // WRITES to production DB — but only ever touches ONE fixed fixture row.
// /api/auth/register upserts by email (see index.js), so re-running this
// updates the same user instead of inserting a new one each time.
// Depends on the Register request above having run at least once so this
// fixture account exists with a known password.
// Store the token so the rest of the 'Requires Real Fixtures' requests in this
// collection can exercise their real 200 happy paths automatically.
pm.test('testEmail not set — still 200 generic message (unknown-email path)', () => {
console.warn('Set collection variable testEmail (a real existing user email) to also cover the reset-token-written / email-sent path.');
pm.test('Generic success message (reset token written, email sent)', () => {
// Uses the same requireUserAuth middleware as /api/wallet — fails before any DB write.
pm.test('authToken not set — expected 401 Missing token', () => {
console.warn('Set collection variable authToken (a real token from POST /api/auth/login) for full coverage.');
pm.test('authToken not set — expected 401 Missing token', () => {
console.warn('Set collection variable authToken for full coverage.');

item.name: Klyme Status by ID — Missing product_ids → 400
Klyme Status by SKU — Missing product_skus → 400
Klyme Status by Name — Missing product_names → 400
By Email — Valid but nonexistent email → 200 empty list
Get by Order Number — Not found → 404
Update Order — Not found → 404 (KNOWN BUG: currently 500)

exec: // KNOWN BUG: the underlying SQL is `UPDATE orders SET ... WHERE order_number = ? LIMIT 1`,
// which is valid MySQL but Postgres does not support LIMIT on UPDATE. The db-adapter.js
// shim does not currently strip it, so this currently 500s with a syntax error instead
// of correctly 404ing. This test asserts the CORRECT behavior and will fail until fixed.

item.name: Delete Order — Not found → 404 (KNOWN BUG: currently 500)

exec: // Same LIMIT-on-DELETE bug as the PUT route above — see collection description.

item.name: Create Order — Empty body → validation error

exec: // Pre-existing app behavior (not a Postgres-conversion artifact): the validation helper
// throws a plain Error for a missing email, which the generic catch-all turns into a 500
// rather than a 400. Documented here as the actual, verified current behavior.
pm.test('Status 500 (validation error, no order created)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(500));
// Klyme credentials ARE configured in this environment, so this exercises the
// orderId/amount validation branch rather than the 'credentials not configured' branch.
// Webhooks always return 200 to the payment provider so it doesn't retry-storm us,
// even when the payload can't be processed.

item.name: Create Payment — API key not configured → 500

exec: // AABANPAY_API_KEY is not set in .env.shared, so this is checked before body validation.
// If AABANPAY_API_KEY is later configured, this test will need updating to expect 400
pm.expect(pm.response.json()).to.deep.equal({ ok: false, error: 'AabanPay API key not configured' });

item.name: Verify Payment — API key not configured → 500

exec: pm.expect(pm.response.json()).to.deep.equal({ ok: false, error: 'AabanPay API key not configured' });
pm.expect(pm.response.json()).to.deep.equal({ ok: false, error: 'AabanPay API key not configured' });
// This route responds with a plain-text body, not JSON.

item.name: Train Model — Upload with no photos → 400
Verify Product — Upload with no photo → 400
Verify Product — Delete with no password → 403

exec: // NOTE: unlike every other request in this collection, this one has a side effect —
// it always accepts and inserts a low-value tracking row (device_fingerprints table)
// even with an empty body. Not a validation endpoint; run sparingly.
// GEMINI_API_KEY is configured in this environment, so this exercises the 'message
// required' validation branch rather than the 503 'AI chat is not configured' branch.
// No row is written and no email is sent — consent check runs before the INSERT.
pm.expect(pm.response.json()).to.have.property('error', 'Consent is required to enter the giveaway.');
// Same handler as /api/fengyu/check, just mounted under a second path.
